mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls over a 3-month period reflects broad economic strength and corporate hiring, leading to higher expected aggregate earnings and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a primary indicator of economic health watched by the Fed and market participants.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low indicates a cyclical downturn is beginning, causing a flight to safety and negative equity returns as recession probabilities are repriced.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month low.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains stagnant, real rates fall, signaling the central bank is behind the curve. This policy error increases uncertainty and compresses multiples, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0.10%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary environments of the 1970s were detrimental to equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative return premium (t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods where only the inflation condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Following a recession, a sharp acceleration in industrial production signals a robust recovery in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, leading to higher earnings expectations and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: The signal has no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) outside of the first 18 months following a NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected positive acceleration in retail sales indicates stronger-than-anticipated consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in near-term earnings forecasts and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger is not statistically different from the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and tighter financial conditions, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month moving average falls below its 12-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates and is a long-leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The signal fails to precede major market drawdowns (>15%) in more than one out of three instances over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms a healthy economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and justifying higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over any rolling 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is at full employment (low unemployment) and the central bank is actively tightening policy, profit margins get squeezed by rising wage and interest costs, leading to negative forward returns as the cycle peaks.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has a dual mandate of price stability and maximum employment.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive average 60-day returns during periods when both trigger conditions are met over a full business cycle.
